# Fatekeeper Guide Library
> Unofficial English Fatekeeper guide hub for Early Access buying decisions, beginner systems, builds, databases, enemies, bosses, and world exploration.

## Core Pages
- [Homepage](https://fatekeeperguide.com): Guide hub with release facts, search demand, source policy, and major guide clusters.
- [Fatekeeper Release Date](https://fatekeeperguide.com/fatekeeper/release-date): Early Access release timing, UTC conversion, platform status, and pre-launch checklist.
- [Fatekeeper Early Access](https://fatekeeperguide.com/fatekeeper/early-access): Content scope, buy/wait risks, update expectations, and launch-week testing plan.
- [Fatekeeper System Requirements](https://fatekeeperguide.com/fatekeeper/system-requirements): CPU, GPU, RAM, DirectX, storage, and conservative PC fit guidance.
- [Fatekeeper Beginner Guide](https://fatekeeperguide.com/fatekeeper/beginner-guide): First-session priorities, combat habits, upgrades, exploration, and early mistakes.
- [Fatekeeper Builds](https://fatekeeperguide.com/fatekeeper/builds): Beginner, spellblade, heavy, dagger, and best-build confidence pages.
- [Fatekeeper Weapons](https://fatekeeperguide.com/fatekeeper/weapons): Weapon database fields, categories, scaling questions, locations, and build links.
- [Fatekeeper Spells](https://fatekeeperguide.com/fatekeeper/spells): Spell roles, mana/cooldown fields, scaling, unlocks, and combo testing.
- [Fatekeeper Bosses](https://fatekeeperguide.com/fatekeeper/bosses): Boss strategy format, attack patterns, rewards, spoiler rules, and verification status.

## Source Policy
- Confirmed facts use Steam, the official Fatekeeper site, THQ Nordic, and official YouTube videos.
- Reddit and media coverage are used for user-demand research and community questions, not final gameplay proof.
- Exact stats, item locations, drops, weaknesses, and build rankings are labeled until tested in the playable Early Access build.

## Key Facts
- Fatekeeper is developed by Paraglacial and published by THQ Nordic.
- The listed platform is Windows PC on Steam.
- Current listed Early Access release timing is June 2, 2026 at 17:00 UTC.
- Steam lists about two hours of content for Early Access launch, about 15 hours planned for 1.0, and about 18 months planned in Early Access.
